Dear Mr. Baugher:
This is to request Commission advice concerning rny duties
under the Political Reforn Act of L974, as amended. This request
is in part made pursuant to Government Code section 831-14 (b).
It is also addressed to your authority as Executive Director,
Chief of Staff and Presidj-ng officer of the Probable Cause
conference.
As you knolr, I was formerly chief of the Enforcenent

I have discussed $rith Acting Enforcement chief, Janis

I have absolutely no recollection of this case. I have no

I understand that Ms. Mclean has no knowledge of any

In
addition, I understand Ms. Mclean has consulted with chief

I encourage you to confer with other present or past employees of

The respondents in this rnatter have asked me to represent

fn your position as Executive Director, you have the
authority to grant a continuance and to control the course of
litigation. 
I therefore request a conti,nuance of the Probable
cause conference until the respondentsr choice of counsel can be
resofved and counsel has tine to become farniliar with the case.
It is also in your role as Executive Director that I ask you
to waive any right the Commission might have to challenge my
representation of these respondents on the basis of my former
ernpJ.oyrnent. This assurnes that you f ind I did not rrpartic j'paterl

in this matter as a state employee within the meaning of

an, that I have no confidential information obtained as a state

The respondents are individual-s of modest neans who need the

Thank you for your consideratj-on of these requests.
truly yours,

sections.question: a) Will you grant the respondents a continuance of the Probable Cause Conference currently scheduled for January 15, 1991, to enable them to secure counsel to represent them? b) Would Government Code sections 87400 and 87401 prohibit me from representing these respondents for compensation in this enforcement proceeding? c) Will the FPPC agree not to move to recuse me on the basis of my prior employment if the respondents choose to retain me following your advice on the preceding questions?
sections.question_synthetic: The requester, a former FPPC Enforcement Division chief, seeks advice on whether he can represent respondents in an enforcement case (88/239) without violating Government Code sections 87400-87401, and requests a continuance and waiver of any FPPC motion to recuse based on his prior employment.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers are present. The numbered requests (a, b, c) form the substantive questions. The letter includes factual background about the requester's prior employment and absence of involvement in the case. Document ID references FPPC No. 88/239 (the enforcement case in question).

embedding.summary: Lawrence A. Haun, former Chief of the FPPC Enforcement Division (resigned July 1988), requests written advice from FPPC Executive Director Gregory Baugher regarding whether he may represent respondents in enforcement case 88/239 without violating conflict of interest statutes, and requests a continuance and advance waiver of any recusal motion.
